_comment: Example AIP Step Execution Token — AIP spec §4.2.3 and §4.6.8. A Step Execution Token is a standard Credential Token whose payload includes aip_approval_id and aip_approval_step (both REQUIRED together). Issued by the Registry to the step actor when the actor claims a claimable step via POST /v1/approvals/{approval_id}/steps/{step_index}/claim. The Relying Party validates it using the standard validation algorithm (§7.3) plus Step 10a (approval step check). Signatures are placeholders.

aip_chain: <compact JWT for Principal Token at delegation_depth=0 — see delegation-chain.json>

_notes.ttl: exp - iat = 300 seconds — Tier 2 scope (transactions) TTL cap. DPoP is REQUIRED for transactions scope per §16.3.
_notes.aip_approval_id_and_step: Both aip_approval_id and aip_approval_step MUST be present together. If one is present without the other, the Relying Party MUST reject with invalid_token.
_notes.aip_approval_step_1based: aip_approval_step=2 is 1-based — it identifies step_index=2 in the Approval Envelope. The Relying Party MUST verify this equals the step it is executing. The path parameter {n} in GET /v1/approvals/{id}/steps/{n} MUST equal this value verbatim.
_notes.relying_party_validation: The Relying Party (payments.bank.example.com) validates: (1) standard 12-step algorithm §7.3, (2) Step 10a: verify aip_approval_id envelope is approved/executing, step 2 is claimed (not already completed), and action_hash matches the expected action, (3) DPoP proof required for transactions scope.
_notes.step_claim_flow: The service agent (payment-processor) calls POST /v1/approvals/apr:7e3b4c0d-.../steps/2/claim. The Registry checks: envelope is approved/executing, step 2 triggered_by=1 is completed, step 2 is pending. If valid, Registry issues this token (status→claimed, claimed_at set) and the agent presents it to payments.bank.example.com.
_notes.engagement_scope: aip_engagement_id links this token to the procurement engagement. The Relying Party MUST verify the engagement is active per §7.3 Step 9e.
_notes.iss_equals_sub: Single-element aip_chain: iss=sub=service agent's AID. Validation Step 8 Post-Check B enforces this.
